description: Update the CDK infrastructure stack to: (1) grant the Lambda function permission to invoke Bedrock (bedrock:InvokeModel), (2) increase the Lambda timeout to accommodate Bedrock and web fetch operations, and (3) add the /recipes/import route to API Gateway.
steps: In apps/infra/src/stacks/recipe-manager-stack.ts, add an IAM policy statement granting the Lambda function bedrock:InvokeModel permission. Use apiHandler.addToRolePolicy() with a PolicyStatement that allows 'bedrock:InvokeModel' on resource 'arn:aws:bedrock:*::foundation-model/eu.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0' (or wildcard for all Bedrock models in the account). Import PolicyStatement from aws-cdk-lib/aws-iam.
Increase the Lambda timeout from Duration.seconds(10) to Duration.seconds(30) to accommodate the time needed for fetching web pages and invoking Bedrock.

Add a new API Gateway resource for /recipes/import: create an 'import' sub-resource under the 'recipes' resource, and add a POST method with the Lambda integration and Cognito authorizer. This must be added BEFORE the {id} resource to avoid conflicts.
Verify the stack synthesizes correctly by running pnpm nx build infra.
acceptance_criteria: Lambda function has IAM permission to call bedrock:InvokeModel
Lambda timeout is increased to 30 seconds
API Gateway has POST /recipes/import route with Cognito authorizer
The /recipes/import resource is added before /recipes/{id} (or as a sibling - CDK handles this correctly since 'import' is a literal and {id} is a greedy proxy)
pnpm nx build infra succeeds without errors
verification: Run pnpm nx build infra and confirm it compiles without errors
findings: Build succeeds with pnpm nx build infra. Added PolicyStatement import from aws-cdk-lib/aws-iam, granted bedrock:InvokeModel on the specific model ARN (arn:aws:bedrock:*::foundation-model/eu.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0), increased timeout to 30s, and added /recipes/import POST route with Cognito authorizer before the {id} resource.
